title: NOC 13112 – Medical Administrative Assistants | GTR Immigration
description: Medical Administrative Assistants support physicians, clinics, hospitals, and healthcare institutions across Canada. Classified under NOC 13112, this occupation continues to experience strong post-pandemic demand and offers practical immigration opportunities through skilled worker programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and employer-supported work permits.

classification.teer: 3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of less than two years; or more than six months of on-the-job training
classification.minor_group: 1311 – Office administrative assistants – general, legal, and medical

common_job_titles: Administrative assistant – medical Medical secretary Ward secretary
duties: Communicate messages between patients and healthcare staff
Prepare and format electronic medical reports and correspondence
Interview patients to complete case histories and documentation
Maintain confidential patient files and records
Prepare meeting agendas and transcribe minutes
Handle billing and prepare financial statements
Establish internal filing and reporting systems
employment_requirements: One- or two-year college programs or specialized courses in medical administration or medical secretarial studies
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 41,100 new positions for Medical Administrative Assistants, compared with 36,400 job seekers—making this a favourable occupation for overseas applicants planning long-term settlement.
additional_information: Medical Administrative Assistants work in hospitals, family practices, diagnostic centres, and specialty clinics nationwide.

Ongoing hiring in healthcare administrationStable front-office and patient-coordination rolesEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported work permit opportunitiesAnnual earnings commonly ranging from $32,200 to $55,900 CAD
